Part B.key_evidence: The central source is point-like in the Science image, absent or much fainter at the exact center in the Template, and appears as a compact positive residual in the Difference image. ndethist=1 and deltajd=0 indicate only a single linked detection so far. The nearest PS1 source is far from the alert position (distpsnr1=15.38 arcsec), so the PS1 star/galaxy information is weak for the transient itself.
Part B.leading_interpretation_and_support: This is most consistent with a real solar-system object (Asteroid benchmark class). With ndethist very small, the required Science-vs-Template check favors motion/offset rather than persistent variability: there is a point source in Science at the candidate center that is not matched by a central counterpart in the Template, producing a clean positive residual. The lack of a nearby host or persistent source at the alert position argues against AGN or a normal stellar variable, and the single-epoch history is more compatible with a mover than with repeated astrophysical variability.
Part B.alternative_analysis: A supernova is the main astrophysical alternative if this is a genuinely new transient on a very faint host, since the residual is compact and positive. However, the cutouts do not show an obvious host at the candidate position, and ndethist=1 with no prior linked activity makes the moving-object interpretation stronger than a host-associated transient here.
